repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Fixes #39224.

Out of scope for this PR but I think we'll want to update the type annotations to include the possibility of these values being `vim.NIL`.

comment_data.body:
I don't think that treating `nil` as equivalent to `vim.NIL` is correct here. The former is meant to represent `symbol.deprecated = nil` while the latter means that `symbol` doesn't have a `deprecated` property. Those are treated differently in LSP and we should handle them differently here as well. This was why we went through the hassle of https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/34849.

`symbol` here is of type `lsp.DocumentSymbol|lsp.SymbolInformation|lsp.WorkspaceSymbol`. The first 2 variants support `deprecated?: boolean`, while `lsp.WorkspaceSymbol` doesn't have a `deprecated` field at all. In the case of `symbol.tags`, the field is either missing or set to an array. The current code would incorrectly accept servers setting `symbol.tags = nil`.

So I would write this as:
```lua
      local is_deprecated = (symbol.deprecated ~= vim.NIL and symbol.deprecated)
        or (symbol.tags ~= vim.NIL and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1964,13 +1965,13 @@ function M.symbols_to_items(symbols, bufnr, position_encoding)
       local end_lnum = range['end'].line + 1
       local end_col = get_line_byte_from_position(bufnr, range['end'], position_encoding) + 1
 
-      local is_deprecated = symbol.deprecated
-        or (symbol.tags and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
+      local is_deprecated = not_nil(symbol.deprecated or nil)

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
        symbol.containerName ~= vim.NIL and ' in ' .. symbol.containerName or '',
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1964,13 +1965,13 @@ function M.symbols_to_items(symbols, bufnr, position_encoding)
       local end_lnum = range['end'].line + 1
       local end_col = get_line_byte_from_position(bufnr, range['end'], position_encoding) + 1
 
-      local is_deprecated = symbol.deprecated
-        or (symbol.tags and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
+      local is_deprecated = not_nil(symbol.deprecated or nil)
+        or (not_nil(symbol.tags) and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
       local text = string.format(
         '[%s] %s%s%s',
         kind,
         symbol.name,
-        symbol.containerName and ' in ' .. symbol.containerName or '',
+        not_nil(symbol.containerName) and ' in ' .. symbol.containerName or '',

comment_data.body:
`not_nil(symbol.deprecated or nil)` is just checking that it's not `nil`, `vim.NIL`, or `false` (hence the `or nil`).

It already accepted servers setting `symbol.tabs == nil` (and `false`). Are you staying that was a bug?
comment_data.diff_hunk:

-        or (symbol.tags and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
+      local is_deprecated = not_nil(symbol.deprecated or nil)

comment_data.body:
This doesn't work. It needs to check both `nil` and `vim.NIL`. For example, lua_ls, with just `symbol.containerName ~= vim.NIL`:

```
vim.schedule callback: /home/liv/src/neovim//runtime/lua/vim/lsp/util.lua:1974: attempt to concatenate field 'containerName' (a nil value)
stack traceback:
	/home/liv/src/neovim//runtime/lua/vim/lsp/util.lua:1974: in function 'map_result'

	/home/liv/src/neovim//runtime/lua/vim/_core/editor.lua:273: in function </home/liv/src/neovim//runtime/lua/vim/_core/editor.lua:272>
```

-      local is_deprecated = symbol.deprecated
-        or (symbol.tags and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
+      local is_deprecated = not_nil(symbol.deprecated or nil)
+        or (not_nil(symbol.tags) and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
       local text = string.format(
         '[%s] %s%s%s',
         kind,
         symbol.name,
-        symbol.containerName and ' in ' .. symbol.containerName or '',
+        not_nil(symbol.containerName) and ' in ' .. symbol.containerName or '',

comment_data.body:
That was `lua-language-server`.

From personally testing the LSPs I have installed, this also applies to:
- `rust-analyzer`
- `json-lsp`
- `fish-lsp`
- `bash-language-server`
- `clangd`

The only language server I have installed that sets `containerName` to `vim.NIL` is `python-lsp-server`.

comment_data.body:
> So I would write this as:
> 
> ```lua
>       local is_deprecated = (symbol.deprecated ~= vim.NIL and symbol.deprecated)
>         or (symbol.tags ~= vim.NIL and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
> ```

Yeah, so this doesn't work either:

```
vim.schedule callback: /home/liv/src/neovim//runtime/lua/vim/_core/shared.lua:336: t: expected table, got nil
stack traceback:
	[C]: in function 'error'

comment_data.body:
I see. My main point is:

> I don't think that treating `nil` as equivalent to `vim.NIL` is correct here. The former is meant to represent `symbol.deprecated = nil` while the latter means that `symbol` doesn't have a `deprecated` property. Those are treated differently in LSP and we should handle them differently here as well. This was why we went through the hassle of #34849.

Depending on the possible types of `symbol` here, we should check if it's `nil` or `vim.NIL`. The current code would incorrectly accept invalid server responses.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1964,13 +1965,13 @@ function M.symbols_to_items(symbols, bufnr, position_encoding)
       local end_lnum = range['end'].line + 1
       local end_col = get_line_byte_from_position(bufnr, range['end'], position_encoding) + 1
 
-      local is_deprecated = symbol.deprecated
-        or (symbol.tags and vim.tbl_contains(symbol.tags, protocol.SymbolTag.Deprecated))
+      local is_deprecated = not_nil(symbol.deprecated or nil)

title: fix(lsp): check for `vim.NIL` in `vim.lsp.util.symbols_to_items()`
